Why Labu Teak

Noble, stable,
and beautiful.

"The queen of hardwoods" — known for its singular shimmer, distinctive grain, and the natural oils that make it virtually indestructible outdoors.

Every tree at Hacienda Labu grows from homogeneous genetic material with standardised maintenance across 3,860 hectares. The result is consistent density and grain across every shipment — year after year.

💧
Weatherproof
High natural oil content makes it impervious to humidity, mould, insects, UV, and temperature change. Beauty conserved for decades.
🔧
Easy to Work
Machines cleanly with sharp tools. Exceptional glue adhesion. Accepts all finishes — teak oil is the standard choice for exterior applications.
✨
Gets Better With Time
The natural jasper and colour of teak deepens with age. An aesthetic that improves — a rarity in any material.
📋
Fully Certified
FSC® C108572 — chain of custody verified from plantation to finished product. Annual independent audit.
🧬
Proprietary Clones
We have developed our own high-performance clones, growing exceptionally well in our microclimate.
🏭
Own Sawmill & Kilns
Two on-site kilns. 10–12% moisture at shipment. 60-day turnaround from accepted order.
Teak Interior — Walls, Ceiling & Floor
Planting Since 2010
18 to 20 years from
seed to final harvest.

First planted in 2010. Thinnings at years 4, 8, 12, and 16 progressively improve timber quality. Final harvest at year 18 to 20 — then immediate replanting. A permanently productive, sustainable system.

The Harvest Cycle

Sustainable.

Every hectare planted in 2010 reaches final harvest between 2028 and 2030. The moment it is harvested, it is replanted. No land sits idle. No forest is lost. The carbon cycle continues uninterrupted.

2010
Planting begins — degraded cattle land transformed into agroforestry plantation in Siuna and Bonanza.
Yr 4
First thinning — removes weaker stems, opens the canopy, generates first commercial timber.
Yr 8
Second thinning — larger-diameter commercial timber. Canopy quality significantly improved.
Yr 12
Third thinning — timber continues to grow, better percentage of hardwood.
Yr 16
Fourth thinning — much better colour, higher percentage of hardwood, good commercial timber.
Yr 18–20
Final harvest — full-diameter premium teak. Immediate replanting begins. The cycle continues in a sustainable manner.
Production Scale

Growing to meet
your volume.

Current production of 15 containers per month is ramping to 60 containers per month by 2030 as additional plantation areas reach maturity.

Now
15 containers/month — consistent, reliable supply available immediately.
2027
Ramp-up as thinning volumes increase across plantation areas.
2030
60 containers/month — full production scale at final harvest.
Application
Fencing & Privacy Screens

Teak's natural oil content and resistance to weathering, moisture, and insects make it the premier choice for pool surrounds, privacy screens, garden boundaries, and architectural dividers.

Fencing Applications

The natural choice
for outdoor precision.

Available in standard board and plank dimensions, or custom-milled to your specified width, thickness, and length. S4S finish available for a clean, installation-ready product.

🏊
Best For
Pool surrounds, garden privacy, architectural screens, boundary fencing.
✨
Finish
Rough or S4S — teak oil recommended for outdoor applications.
📐
Custom
Custom dimensions milled to order. 60-day turnaround from accepted order.
From The Craftsmen

Those who work teak
choose teak.

Three professionals — a designer, a carpenter, and a cabinetmaker — with a combined 95 years of working teak.

"

The natural resin makes it extremely weather-resistant. Once assembled, it doesn't move or crack. Knowing how to work teak and applying the right finish, you get a high-quality product that lasts for years.

Gustavo Espinoza
Creative & Design Director · Hecho Design
20 years working with teak
"

One of the best qualities of teak is the jasper it gives — the colours. You just apply wood stain and the beauty of the piece is incomparable, magnificent.

Rafael López
Carpenter & Cabinetmaker
40 years of experience
"

The highlight of teak is the jasper. It also has good contrast — the texture is naturally beautiful and its colour is serene. That's what I call it, and that's what I love most about working with teak.

José Cano Gaitán
Cabinetmaker
35 years of experience
Technical Specifications

The numbers behind
the reputation.

1,005Kp/mm²Janka Hardness (Kiln-Dried)
800Kg/m³Air-Dry Density
3.5lbsWeight per Board Foot
10–12%Moisture (Kiln-Dried)

Teak accepts all standard finishes. Teak oil is the standard choice for exterior applications. Exceptional glue adhesion. Sharp tools recommended.

PropertyUnitSanta MariaTeak ★Quebracho
Green densityKg/m³9601,100—
Air-dry densityKg/m³7008001,240
Modulus of ruptureKg/cm²6368301,400
Modulus of elasticityKg/cm²94,623105,313154,000
Janka hardnessKp/mm²502440–1,005900

★ Quebracho is harder but its extreme density limits workability. Teak's balance of strength, oil content, and machinability is unmatched for projects demanding both performance and finish quality.

Products & Dimensions

Standard range.
Custom on request.

All products available rough or S4S. Custom lengths and dimensions available. Consistent supply year-round from 3,860 ha of managed plantation.

The Sawmill · Siuna, Nicaragua
Nicaragua's highest-capacity
teak operation.

High-technology machinery and two kilns — ensuring every shipment leaves at the correct moisture for long-term stability. Custom milling to order. Full S4S finishing. 60 days from accepted order.

15–30Containers per month — consistent supply guaranteed year-round
2Kilns with capacity of 8 containers of kiln-dried lumber per month
10–12%Moisture content on delivery — optimal range for dimensional stability
2013FSC® certified — both plantation and sawmill. Chain of custody verified
60Days turnaround from accepted order — custom dimensions & S4S available
How to Buy

Simple. Direct.
No middlemen.

01
Submit Your Inquiry
Use the form below. Tell us: product type, grade, volume (m³), and delivery destination. The more detail, the faster we can quote.
02
We Send a Quote
Our commercial team sends pricing, lead times, and shipping options. We can arrange delivery or quote FOB Corinto for your freight forwarder.
03
Confirm & We Prepare
Once confirmed, we prepare your order — kiln-dried to spec, graded, and documented. Full FSC chain-of-custody and EUDR compliance paperwork included.
04
Delivery
Delivery to your warehouse across Nicaragua and Central America. International orders: FOB Corinto. Alternative ports available on request.
Shipping Port
FOB Corinto, Nicaragua · Alternative ports available on request
Minimum Order
No minimum for local orders · International: contact us for volume requirements
Documentation Included
FSC® C108572 · EUDR Declaration · ISPM-15 · Phytosanitary Certificate · Commercial Invoice
